---
name: backend-api-usage
description: How to use the PubWiki backend API from frontend applications, including authentication, type-safe requests, and best practices.
---

# Backend API Usage Skill

This document explains how to use the PubWiki backend API from frontend applications.

## API Documentation

The complete API specification is defined in OpenAPI format:

- **OpenAPI spec**: packages/api/openapi.yaml
- **Bundled spec**: `packages/api/openapi.bundled.yaml` (generated)
- **Generated types**: `packages/api/src/generated/openapi.ts`
- **Validation schemas**: `packages/api/src/validate/schemas.ts` (Zod schemas generated by orval)

To regenerate types after modifying the OpenAPI spec:
```bash
cd packages/api && pnpm generate
```

## Overview

The backend API is served by the Hub service (Cloudflare Workers). Frontend applications interact with it through:

1. **`@pubwiki/api/client`** - Type-safe API client based on openapi-fetch
2. **Better-Auth client** - For authentication operations

## Quick Start

### 1. Import Clients

```typescript
import { createAuthClient, createApiClient } from '@pubwiki/api/client';
import { API_BASE_URL } from '$lib/config'; // Your app's config

// Auth client - for login/register/session
const authClient = createAuthClient('https://api.pubwiki.com');

// API client - for all other API calls
const apiClient = createApiClient(`${API_BASE_URL}`); // includes /api suffix
```

### 2. Authentication Flow

```typescript
// Register
await authClient.signUp.email({
  email: 'user@example.com',
  password: 'password123',
  name: 'Display Name',
  username: 'username',
});

// Login with email
await authClient.signIn.email({
  email: 'user@example.com',
  password: 'password123',
});

// Login with username
await authClient.signIn.username({
  username: 'username',
  password: 'password123',
});

// Get current session
const session = await authClient.getSession();

// Logout
await authClient.signOut();
```

After login, session cookies are automatically managed. The API client includes `credentials: 'include'` to send cookies with requests.

### 3. Making API Requests

```typescript
// GET request with query params
const { data, error } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts', {
  params: {
    query: {
      page: 1,
      limit: 20,
      sortBy: 'createdAt',
      sortOrder: 'desc',
    },
  },
});

// GET request with path params
const { data, error } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/graph', {
  params: {
    path: { artifactId: 'uuid-here' },
    query: { version: 'latest' },
  },
});

// POST with JSON body
const { data, error } = await apiClient.POST('/projects', {
  body: {
    name: 'My Project',
    slug: 'my-project',
    topic: 'general',
  },
});

// DELETE
const { data, error } = await apiClient.DELETE('/artifacts/{artifactId}', {
  params: { path: { artifactId: 'uuid-here' } },
});
```

## Type Safety

### Importing Types

All API types are exported from `@pubwiki/api`:

```typescript
import type {
  // Response types
  ArtifactListItem,
  ProjectDetail,
  DiscussionDetail,
  Pagination,
  ApiError,
  
  // Request body types
  CreateProjectMetadata,
  CreateDiscussionRequest,
  
  // Query param types
  ArtifactSortBy,
  SortOrder,
} from '@pubwiki/api';
```

### Response Types by Operation

```typescript
import type {
  ListArtifactsResponse,
  CreateArtifactResponse,
  GetArtifactGraphResponse,
  ListDiscussionsResponse,
  CreateProjectResponse,
  // ... etc
} from '@pubwiki/api';
```

## Error Handling

### Pattern 1: Check error field

```typescript
const { data, error } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts');

if (error) {
  // error has type { error: string }
  console.error('API error:', error.error);
  return;
}

// data is typed correctly
console.log(data.artifacts);
```

### Pattern 2: Try-catch for network errors

```typescript
try {
  const { data, error } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts');
  
  if (error) {
    this.error = error.error || 'Failed to fetch';
    return { success: false, error: this.error };
  }
  
  return { success: true, data };
} catch (e) {
  // Network error, timeout, etc.
  this.error = e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Unknown error';
  return { success: false, error: this.error };
}
```

### Common HTTP Status Codes

| Status | Meaning | When it occurs |
|--------|---------|----------------|
| 200 | Success | Request succeeded |
| 201 | Created | Resource created successfully |
| 204 | No Content | Delete succeeded |
| 400 | Bad Request | Invalid request body/params |
| 401 | Unauthorized | Not logged in |
| 403 | Forbidden | No permission for resource |
| 404 | Not Found | Resource doesn't exist |
| 409 | Conflict | Concurrent modification |

## Best Practices

### 1. Create Singleton Client

```typescript
// lib/api.ts
import { createApiClient } from '@pubwiki/api/client';
import { API_BASE_URL } from '$lib/config';

export const apiClient = createApiClient(API_BASE_URL);
```

### 2. Parallel Requests

Fetch independent data in parallel:

```typescript
const [homepageResult, graphResult, lineageResult] = await Promise.all([
  apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/homepage', {
    params: { path: { artifactId } },
    parseAs: 'text',
  }),
  apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/graph', {
    params: { path: { artifactId } },
  }),
  apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/lineage', {
    params: { path: { artifactId } },
  }),
]);
```

### 3. Caching in Stores

```typescript
class ArtifactStore {
  private cache = new Map<string, ArtifactDetails>();
  
  async fetchDetails(id: string) {
    // Return cached if available
    const cached = this.cache.get(id);
    if (cached?.graph) return cached;
    
    // Fetch and cache
    const { data } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/graph', {
      params: { path: { artifactId: id } },
    });
    
    if (data) {
      this.cache.set(id, { ...cached, graph: data });
    }
    return this.cache.get(id);
  }
}
```

### 4. Form Data Uploads (Multipart)

For file uploads (artifacts, saves), use FormData:

```typescript
const formData = new FormData();

// Add JSON metadata
formData.append('metadata', JSON.stringify({
  artifactId: 'uuid',
  name: 'My Artifact',
  // ... other fields
}));

// Add binary files with content-addressed keys
formData.append('vfs[SHA256_HASH]', tarGzBlob);
formData.append('save[SHA256_HASH]', quadsBlob);

// Custom fetch for multipart
const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE_URL}/artifacts`, {
  method: 'POST',
  body: formData,
  credentials: 'include',
});
```

### 5. Non-JSON Responses

```typescript
// HTML response
const { data } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts/{artifactId}/homepage', {
  params: { path: { artifactId } },
  parseAs: 'text',  // Important for non-JSON
});

// Binary response
const { data } = await apiClient.GET('/saves/{commit}/data', {
  params: { path: { commit } },
  parseAs: 'blob',
});
```

## API Endpoint Reference

### Public Endpoints (No Auth Required)

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Health check |
| GET | `/artifacts` | List public artifacts |
| GET | `/artifacts/{id}/homepage` | Get artifact homepage HTML |
| GET | `/artifacts/{id}/graph` | Get artifact node graph |
| GET | `/artifacts/{id}/lineage` | Get version lineage |
| GET | `/projects` | List public projects |
| GET | `/projects/{id}` | Get project details |
| GET | `/users/{username}` | Get user profile |

### Authenticated Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/me` | Get current user |
| PUT | `/me` | Update profile |
| POST | `/artifacts` | Create artifact |
| PATCH | `/artifacts` | Create new version |
| DELETE | `/artifacts/{id}` | Delete artifact |
| POST | `/projects` | Create project |
| DELETE | `/projects/{id}` | Delete project |
| POST | `/saves` | Create save |
| DELETE | `/saves/{commit}` | Delete save |

### Discussion Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/discussions` | List discussions |
| POST | `/discussions` | Create discussion |
| PATCH | `/discussions/{id}` | Update discussion |
| DELETE | `/discussions/{id}` | Delete discussion |
| POST | `/discussions/{id}/replies` | Add reply |

## Common Patterns

### Loading State Management

```typescript
class Store {
  loading = $state(false);
  error = $state<string | null>(null);
  
  async fetch() {
    this.loading = true;
    this.error = null;
    
    try {
      const { data, error } = await apiClient.GET('/endpoint');
      if (error) {
        this.error = error.error;
        return;
      }
      // Use data
    } catch (e) {
      this.error = e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Unknown error';
    } finally {
      this.loading = false;
    }
  }
}
```

### Optimistic Updates

```typescript
async function deleteItem(id: string) {
  // Optimistically remove from UI
  const backup = this.items;
  this.items = this.items.filter(i => i.id !== id);
  
  const { error } = await apiClient.DELETE('/items/{id}', {
    params: { path: { id } },
  });
  
  if (error) {
    // Rollback on failure
    this.items = backup;
    return { success: false, error: error.error };
  }
  
  return { success: true };
}
```

## Validation Schemas

For client-side validation, use Zod schemas from `@pubwiki/api/validate`:

```typescript
import { CreateProjectBody, ListArtifactsQueryParams } from '@pubwiki/api/validate';

// Validate before sending
const result = CreateProjectBody.safeParse(formData);
if (!result.success) {
  console.error(result.error.issues);
  return;
}

// Use validated data
await apiClient.POST('/projects', { body: result.data });
```

## Debugging

### Check Request/Response

```typescript
const { data, error, response } = await apiClient.GET('/artifacts');

console.log('Status:', response.status);
console.log('Headers:', Object.fromEntries(response.headers));
console.log('Data:', data);
console.log('Error:', error);
```

### Session Issues

If requests return 401 when you expect to be logged in:

1. Check cookies in browser DevTools
2. Verify `credentials: 'include'` is set
3. Check CORS configuration allows credentials
4. Try `await authClient.getSession()` to verify session state
